name: Mushroom Tartlets
recipeIngredient: 1 cup unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
1 pound mushrooms (including 1/2 oz cepes or boletus mushrooms)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl or pastry board, combine flour and salt. Add the small pieces of butter to the flour mixture.
2. Make a well in the center and pour in half of the water. Mix with your fingers until the dough is crumbly but holds together when pressed.
3. Gradually add more water if needed until the dough comes together without being kneaded.
4. Form the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic, and chill for at least 1 hour.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. Roll out the chilled dough on a floured surface and press it into tartlet shells or lined tart tin casings, trimming any excess.
7. Cover the mushrooms with water and let soak for 20 minutes, then drain well and finely mince.
8. In a skillet over medium heat, melt the butter. Add the mushrooms and cepes, sautéing until the moisture evaporates completely.
9. Remove from heat and set aside.
10. Reduce the reserved liquid by half, remove from heat, and cool slightly.
11. Combine the reduced liquid, cream, white wine, and thyme in a bowl. Season with salt and white pepper to taste. Mix well.
12. Reduce again slightly and let cool before adding eggs one at a time until just combined.
13. Arrange mushrooms in the tart shells.
14. Pour the egg mixture over the mushrooms.
15. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until set and golden crust forms on top.
16. Remove from oven and let cool briefly before cutting and serving warm or at room temperature.
